The '''''Harasser''''' [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] consisted of two [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigates]]—designated '''''Harasser 1''''' and '''''Harasser 2'''''—that served in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. They were part of a [[Task force/Legends|task force]] led by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']] during [[Operation Strike Fear]]. While mustering in the [[Dellalt system/Legends|Dellalt system]], the ''Harasser'' group was identified by a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] reconnaissance mission flown by [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] in a lone [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]].

==Characteristics==
Both ''Harasser 1'' and ''Harasser 2'' were fitted with [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries and [[Hangar/Legends|hangar bays]], and were painted the standard [[gray]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] and marked with two small [[white]] Imperial emblems on each side of the bow and one small [[white]] one on each side of the stern of each ship.

==Behind the scenes==
In the ''[[Child of Light]]'' [[roleplaying game]] adventure written by [[Lee Pickler]] and released by the [[RPGA]] on behalf of [[Wizards of the Coast]], a ''Harasser'' group Nebulon-B escort frigate enters the [[Dalicron system/Legends|Dalicron system]] and places it under quarantine while the player characters are attempting to take a strongly [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] child, named [[Caeleb]], into Alliance protection.

The ''Harasser'' [[Battle group/Legends|group]] was represented by a single Nebulon-B frigate miniature in the [[Star Wars PocketModel TCG: Base Set|initial release]] of the ''[[Star Wars PocketModel TCG]]''. The fact that a single unit is labeled ''Harasser''—rather than two separate ships separately identified as ''Harasser 1'' and ''Harasser 2''—is irrelevant due to the allowance of the use of multiple copies of units by a player in the game.
